title: castilleja angustifolia subsp. abbreviata
intro: Castilleja angustifolia subsp. abbreviata, also known as Abbreviated Indian Paintbrush, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright red and yellow flowers that resemble a paintbrush. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to prepare herbal remedies, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures to reap its health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating pain. However, further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential interactions of Castilleja angustifolia subsp. abbreviata.
